title: Powerhouse architecture has three layers: Data Infrastructure (Reactor), Host Application (Connect/Switchboard/Fusion), and Customization (plugins)
content: Powerhouse's software architecture consists of three layers: (1) **Data Infrastructure Layer** -- the Reactor as storage node supporting multiple adapters (local, cloud, decentralized like IPFS), plus network components (event buses, queues, caching, load balancers); (2) **Host Application Layer** -- empty shell applications gaining functionality through plugins: Connect (contributor tools), Switchboard (scalable API service for read models), Fusion (public-facing collaboration/marketplaces), Renown (decentralized auth/reputation), Academy (onboarding/training); (3) **Customization Layer** -- organization-specific plugins for each host application. This modular approach means Connect plugins provide tailored contributor apps, Switchboard plugins aggregate data into specialized read models, and Fusion plugins build custom marketplaces.
description: The Reactor handles storage/sync, host apps are plugin shells, and the customization layer provides organization-specific extensions.
